name: ecommerce_product_scraper
title: E-commerce Product Scraper
description: Extract clean product metadata from a public product page, including title, price, currency, availability, image, brand, SKU, rating, review count, and description when visible.

seo.title: E-commerce Product Scraper Tool for Price and Product Data
seo.description: Extract structured product data from public ecommerce pages with Better Fetch, including title, price, currency, availability, images, brand, SKU, ratings, and description.
seo.intro: E-commerce Product Scraper turns a public product detail page into normalized product metadata for monitoring, catalog enrichment, competitive research, and agent workflows. It renders the target through Better Fetch, prefers standards-based JSON-LD Product markup, and falls back to Open Graph, product meta tags, itemprop attributes, and common storefront HTML patterns.

useCases.title: Price monitoring
useCases.description: Track public product pages over time and return normalized price, currency, availability, and final URL fields for comparison or alerting.

useCases.title: Catalog enrichment
useCases.description: Pull product titles, images, brand names, SKUs, descriptions, ratings, and review counts into an internal product database or agent context.

useCases.title: Competitive research
useCases.description: Let an agent inspect comparable product pages without hand-copying page text, price blocks, and visible product metadata.

faqs.question: Does E-commerce Product Scraper work on every store?
faqs.answer: It works best on public product detail pages that expose JSON-LD Product data, Open Graph product tags, itemprop attributes, or common visible price and availability markup.

faqs.question: Does the tool need store API keys?
faqs.answer: No. The tool reads public page content through Better Fetch and does not use private store APIs, carts, checkout sessions, or account-specific credentials.

faqs.question: What happens when a field is not visible?
faqs.answer: Missing values are omitted from the output rather than guessed, so downstream systems can distinguish visible data from unavailable metadata.

max_description_chars.description: Maximum number of characters to keep from the product description

currency.description: ISO currency code or visible currency marker
